review_text: Been buying Rockports for work and also buying the cheaper New Balance shoes with very little success in comfort they started off okay, but after a month started hurting.  When I finally decided to spend more for my comfort, I wasn't disappointed.  I read the reviews regarding the MR1123's and decided to buy them online; Man, I wasn't disappointed! These are the most comfortable shoes I've had in a very long time.  I liked them so much that I decided to buy them for my father-in-law who also has difficulty.  BUY THEM!  You'll love them.
It is a very pretty ring although I am petite I like big rings, this was a nice ring and very classy.

I got this package on time. The shoes are good. Very good for trail running. I am just a greenhorn, so I cannot add anything more than that.Cons: The only disadvantages I face are:1. The laces go away all the time, so I have to stop in the middle of my run to tie them which can be annoying sometimes.Other than that no complaints.

This was actually a review I looked forward to writing. I love the ring; it's lovely & I've already recd compliments. But the one surprise I wasn't expecting is the way the ring band itself  (& Im assuming this was a design feature & not a mistake!) is 'slightly crimped' to square off two sides so when you slip it on your finger, if you twist it a bit to the 11o'clock position, it glides over your knuckle easily, and when full on & back at high noon position, it sits and feels Very comfortable!As I said, I'm assuming this was intentional; nevertheless, it works very well if your knuckles make your true ring size a bit of a tight squeeze to get over! I don't have large knuckles - by a long shot - but I have noticed some small changes as I get older which often requires either some discomfort or cold water & soap to assist getting my rings to slide over the knuckle. So, if you do as well, you might even wish to make this adjustment yourself on any rings which cause this problem (or a jeweler can always do it for you).Oh, did I say the ring was lovely? ;-D

Bought these boots for my daughter for Christmas.  According to the reviews, they run big so I bought her a size down and they fit perfectly.  She loved the polka dots and ended up wearing the boots all through Christmas.  If there is one drawback, it is that the inside is a little rough, so an insole would be a great improvement.
Well first of all it is much smaller than I thought and the gold is so flimsy that it bends easily not enough gold to hold it from bending. I gave it to my niece because it bends to easy for me.
First pair if flip flops that don't make the flip flop floppy noise. Very light weigh easy to wear no tear on foot and feel broken in the first day. Very comfortable.
My last pair went down to the bottom of Coronado Bay and I went 2 years without Ray-Bans...I put them on and went "AAAHHHHHHhhhh..." :)

My sister is a pink loving girlie girl. She absolutely loved this! I was a little wary of the purchase because of the steeply discounted price, but highly impressed when I received the product! It came in a very nice box with a clasp, protected inside a gift box, all tucked into a lovely draw string satchel along with the certificate of authenticity. The pink sapphire has beautiful sparkle and is framed beautifully by the shining accent gems. The chain, while still dainty, is actually a decent size and sturdy as well. My sister's jaw dropped as she gasped with delight at her new treasure. It was the reaction I was hoping for. I was so happy she loved this as much as I hoped she would. I couldn't be happier with this purchase!

I thought they looked great but they were not very comfortable. There was too much of a downward slope. I wore them to work but they were more of a "going out" shoe. Also I thought they ran big. I usually wear a 7- 7.5 but felt like I should have gotten a 6.5 in these. If you just need a pair of shoes for a night out and don't think you will walk to much then these will look good but would not recommend wearing for long periods.

Well, here I am.  Runner girl.  Yoga and weight lifting twice a week too, but after three kids I cannot seem to get my little tummy pooch to go away entirely.  I have a gray Athleta sweater dress that I love EXCEPT for the tummy pooch.  Enter SPANX.  This thing is the bomb.  I wore it twice in the last week and both times I felt like a rock star.  I wore it with my gray sweater dress, and the very gay flight attendant on my recent Delta flight took one look at me as I boarded and said, "OH!  You look FABULOUS.  Really.  Well done!"  Made my day.  Alright, made my week.  As for the potty thing, the little slit in the bottom works fine.  Even in an airplane bathroom.  As I say, I wore it for two days running all over the country, and no complaints.  A little weird the first time you try it, but fine after you get the hang of it.  The only discomfort I had was at the front of my shoulders where the straps from the Spanx came up against the straps of my bra.  After 12 hours in it one day, I had little red marks where the skin had occasionally gotten pinched.  For as gorgeous as I felt, I didn't mind.  One last comment:  I have tried the shorts versions of Spanx and they inevitably roll down at the waist causing an extra tight, rolled up line of elastic cutting off breathing and circulation and leaving a roll of fat above.  With the Bodysuit, this obviously cannot happen.  Smmmmmoooooth all around.  Get it.  It's worth the $$$.

I ordered this hat for my 9-yr old son who is a huge Adventure Time fan and plans to be Finn for Halloween.  This product meets all the criteria for a hat, so can't really complain there.  BUT, the seams on the top are not right for Finn.  The ears are set so far back that they cannot really be seen from a front view.  I know it's a cartoon, but Finn's hat is more or less like a rectangle with ears, so you'd think there would be one seam running across the top, like a pillowcase.  This hat is more like a bucket hat in that there is a top panel of fleece sewn to the sidewall of the hat, and then the ears come out of that.  So, again, great as a hat, but doesn't really read as Finn's hat.  Perhaps it would be better on an adult, though the fit is adequate for my son, and I am skeptical as I feel there is a fundamental design flaw.  If you are considering this hat for anyone who is in the least bit particular about "accuracy," you may want to find another design or even make your own for about 5.00 worth of fleece.  Have to acknowledge good experience with seller and the hat does seem to be made well, but just not quite right.

Allegra K can be a bit hit or miss with quality, but if your expectations are for an inexpensive but cool looking pair of arm sleeves they are better than expected. The arm and thumb holes are reinforced and elastics are of good quality for the price, I'm not expecting these to last for years and years but I'd repurchase them if they wore out or got damaged.  There were a few loose threads, but nothing that impacted the integrity of the sleeve, just loose ends. I am an adult with medium sized arms (12 petite in clothes and 5'5 tall) and these stay up well without cutting off circulation; but, if you are heavier, very slender, or have very short arms these might not suit your needs.  I'm happy with the purchase, but I am used to wearing thumb hole sleeves which some people find uncomfortable.

I admit I was nervous ordering a bikini with firm cups that comes in s/m/l/xl sizing, since while I'm tall & kind of curvy, I'm more pear/shaped with a smaller bust, & I was nervous of a pre-molded bust. It fit fine & is flattering. Since I got an XL & I'm barely a c-cup, word of warning for very busty girls!My only complaint, & this bugged me about other iron fist bikinis, the neck straps are all the way in your arm pit almost, providing a higher possibility of falling out from the middle, since there isn't any support except from the far sides which is weird-feeling. This bikini is still much more comfortable than the bela Lugosi bandeau bikini I also have from iron fist, but I wonder why they keep the straps up in the armpit. An inch closer to the middle wouldn't change the design or halter style, but would make even my small boobs feel so much better supported. Otherwise, excellent stuff as always from iron fist.
